channel.title: Headroom Studio - Notes from the studio
channel.description: Monitoring discipline, hearing health, and the small macOS tools that make studio work easier.

item.title: The noise floor problem - why audiobooks get rejected on the quiet parts
item.description: The noise floor is the ACX gate narrators trip over - the room tone in the gaps between phrases. Measuring it means reading the silence, not the speech, which is harder than it sounds.

item.title: Wiring Lyra into every Mac automation surface
item.description: App Intents, Focus Filters, x-callback URLs. One change underneath, several different shapes on top.

item.title: The musician tinnitus number is 43%
item.description: A 2026 meta-analysis of 28,000+ musicians puts hard numbers on what audiology has been quietly saying for decades.

item.title: Calibrate once, recall forever - pairing Audita and Lyra
item.description: A pink-noise sweep, a name, and one keystroke replace fifteen years of guessing what "Mix" actually means in dB SPL.

item.title: The 3 dB rule - the single number that explains studio loudness
item.description: Every 3 dB increase halves your safe listening time. Every 3 dB decrease doubles it.

item.title: Monitor at a number, not a vibe - the case for calibrated studio listening
item.description: Translation, consistency, lower hearing dose, less fatigue - the case for calibrated studio listening.
